1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class MergeRequest < ApplicationRecord
4:   extend Gitlab::Cache::RequestCache
5: 
6:   include AtomicInternalId
7:   include IidRoutes
8:   include Issuable
9:   include Noteable
10:   include Referable
11:   include Presentable
12:   include TimeTrackable
13:   include ManualInverseAssociation
14:   include EachBatch
15:   include ThrottledTouch
16:   include Gitlab::Utils::StrongMemoize
17:   include LabelEventable
18:   include ReactiveCaching
19:   include FromUnion
20:   include DeprecatedAssignee
21:   include ShaAttribute
22:   include MilestoneEventable
23:   include StateEventable
24:   include Approvable
25:   include IdInOrdered
26:   include Todoable
27:   include Spammable
28: 
29:   extend ::Gitlab::Utils::Override
30: 
31:   sha_attribute :squash_commit_sha
32:   sha_attribute :merge_ref_sha
33: 
34:   self.reactive_cache_key = ->(model) { [model.project.id, model.iid] }
35:   self.reactive_cache_refresh_interval = 10.minutes
36:   self.reactive_cache_lifetime = 10.minutes
37:   self.reactive_cache_work_type = :no_dependency
38: 
39:   SORTING_PREFERENCE_FIELD = :merge_requests_sort
40:   CI_MERGE_REQUEST_DESCRIPTION_MAX_LENGTH = 2700
41:   MERGE_LEASE_TIMEOUT = 15.minutes.to_i
42: 
43:   belongs_to :target_project, class_name: "Project"
44:   belongs_to :source_project, class_name: "Project"
45:   belongs_to :merge_user, class_name: "User"
46: 
47:   has_internal_id :iid, scope: :target_project, track_if: -> { !importing? },
48:     init: ->(mr, scope) do
49:       if mr
50:         mr.target_project&.merge_requests&.maximum(:iid)
51:       elsif scope[:project]
52:         where(target_project: scope[:project]).maximum(:iid)
53:       end
54:     end
55: 
56:   has_many :merge_request_diffs,
57:     -> { regular }, inverse_of: :merge_request
58:   has_many :merge_request_context_commits, inverse_of: :merge_request
59:   has_many :merge_request_context_commit_diff_files, through: :merge_request_context_commits, source: :diff_files
60: 
61:   has_many :generated_ref_commits,
62:     primary_key: [:iid, :target_project_id],
63:     class_name: 'MergeRequests::GeneratedRefCommit',
64:     foreign_key: [:merge_request_iid, :project_id],
65:     inverse_of: :merge_request
66: 
67:   has_one :merge_request_diff,
68:     -> { regular.order('merge_request_diffs.id DESC') }, inverse_of: :merge_request
69:   # Enabling inverse relationship causes previously cached merge_head_diff to be returned as merge_request_diff
70:   # MR: https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/merge_requests/214072
71:   has_one :merge_head_diff,
72:     -> { merge_head }, inverse_of: false, class_name: 'MergeRequestDiff'
73:   has_one :cleanup_schedule, inverse_of: :merge_request
74:   has_one :predictions, inverse_of: :merge_request
75:   delegate :suggested_reviewers, to: :predictions
76: 
77:   has_one :merge_schedule, class_name: 'MergeRequests::MergeSchedule', inverse_of: :merge_request
78:   has_one :metrics, inverse_of: :merge_request, autosave: true
79:   # NOTE: We do not want to autosave merge_data as this is saved manually
80:   has_one :merge_data, class_name: 'MergeRequests::MergeData', inverse_of: :merge_request, foreign_key: :merge_request_id, autosave: false
81: 
82:   belongs_to :latest_merge_request_diff, class_name: 'MergeRequestDiff'
83:   manual_inverse_association :latest_merge_request_diff, :merge_request
84: 
85:   # method overridden in EE
86:   def suggested_reviewer_users
87:     User.none
88:   end
89: 
90:   # This is the same as latest_merge_request_diff unless:
91:   # 1. There are arguments - in which case we might be trying to force-reload.
92:   # 2. This association is already loaded.
93:   # 3. The latest diff does not exist.
94:   # 4. It doesn't have any merge_request_diffs - it returns an empty MergeRequestDiff
95:   #
96:   # The second one in particular is important - MergeRequestDiff#merge_request
97:   # is the inverse of MergeRequest#merge_request_diff, which means it may not be
98:   # the latest diff, because we could have loaded any diff from this particular
99:   # MR. If we haven't already loaded a diff, then it's fine to load the latest.
100:   def merge_request_diff
101:     fallback = latest_merge_request_diff unless association(:merge_request_diff).loaded?
102: 
103:     fallback || super || MergeRequestDiff.new(merge_request_id: id)
104:   end
105: 
106:   belongs_to :head_pipeline, class_name: "Ci::Pipeline", inverse_of: :merge_requests_as_head_pipeline
107: 
108:   has_many :events, as: :target, dependent: :delete_all # rubocop:disable Cop/ActiveRecordDependent
109: 
110:   has_many :merge_request_issues,
111:     class_name: 'MergeRequestsClosingIssues',
112:     inverse_of: :merge_request,
113:     dependent: :delete_all # rubocop:disable Cop/ActiveRecordDependent
114: 
115:   has_many :merge_request_closing_issues,
116:     -> { link_type_closes },
117:     class_name: 'MergeRequestsClosingIssues',
118:     inverse_of: :merge_request
119: 
120:   has_one :approval_metrics,
121:     class_name: 'MergeRequest::ApprovalMetrics',
122:     inverse_of: :merge_request
123: 
124:   has_many :cached_closes_issues,
125:     through: :merge_request_closing_issues,
126:     source: :issue
127:   has_many :pipelines_for_merge_request, foreign_key: 'merge_request_id', class_name: 'Ci::Pipeline', inverse_of: :merge_request
128:   has_many :suggestions, through: :notes
129:   has_many :unresolved_notes, -> { unresolved }, as: :noteable, class_name: 'Note', inverse_of: :noteable
130: 
131:   has_many :merge_request_assignees
132:   has_many :assignees, class_name: "User", through: :merge_request_assignees
133:   has_many :merge_request_reviewers
134:   has_many :reviewers, class_name: "User", through: :merge_request_reviewers
135:   has_many :user_mentions, class_name: "MergeRequestUserMention", dependent: :delete_all # rubocop:disable Cop/ActiveRecordDependent
136: 
137:   has_many :deployment_merge_requests
138: 
139:   # These are deployments created after the merge request has been merged, and
140:   # the merge request was tracked explicitly (instead of implicitly using a CI
141:   # build).
142:   has_many :deployments,
143:     through: :deployment_merge_requests
144: 
145:   has_many :draft_notes
146:   has_many :reviews, inverse_of: :merge_request
147:   has_many :reviewed_by_users, -> { distinct }, through: :reviews, source: :author
148:   has_many :created_environments, class_name: 'Environment', foreign_key: :merge_request_id, inverse_of: :merge_request
149:   has_many :assignment_events, class_name: 'ResourceEvents::MergeRequestAssignmentEvent', inverse_of: :merge_request
150: 
151:   KNOWN_MERGE_PARAMS = [
152:     :auto_merge_strategy,
153:     :should_remove_source_branch,
154:     :force_remove_source_branch,
155:     :commit_message,
156:     :squash_commit_message,
157:     :sha,
158:     :skip_ci
159:   ].freeze
160:   serialize :merge_params, type: Hash # rubocop:disable Cop/ActiveRecordSerialize
161: 
162:   before_validation :set_draft_status
163: 
164:   after_create :ensure_merge_request_diff, unless: :skip_ensure_merge_request_diff
165:   after_update :clear_memoized_shas
166:   after_update :reload_diff_if_branch_changed
167:   after_save :keep_around_commit, unless: :importing?
168:   after_save :save_merge_data_changes
169:   after_commit :enqueue_keep_around_commit, unless: :importing?
170:   after_commit :ensure_metrics!, on: [:create, :update], unless: :importing?
171:   after_commit :expire_etag_cache, unless: :importing?
172: 
173:   after_find :preload_branches
174: 
175:   # When this attribute is true some MR validation is ignored
176:   # It allows us to close or modify broken merge requests
177:   attr_accessor :allow_broken, :skip_branch_existence_check
178: 
179:   # Temporary flag to skip merge_request_diff creation on create.
180:   # See https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/merge_requests/100390
181:   attr_accessor :skip_ensure_merge_request_diff
182: 
183:   # Temporary fields to store compare vars
184:   # when creating new merge request
185:   attr_accessor :can_be_created, :compare_commits, :diff_options, :compare
186: 
187:   # Flag to skip triggering mergeRequestMergeStatusUpdated GraphQL subscription.
188:   attr_accessor :skip_merge_status_trigger
189: 
190:   attr_spammable :title, spam_title: true
191:   attr_spammable :description, spam_description: true
192: 
193:   participant :reviewers
194: 
195:   # Keep states definition to be evaluated before the state_machine block to
196:   #   avoid spec failures. If this gets evaluated after, the `merged` and `locked`
197:   #   states (which are overridden) can be nil.
198:   #
199:   def self.available_state_names
200:     super + [:merged, :locked]
201:   end
202: 
203:   state_machine :state_id, initial: :opened, initialize: false do
204:     event :close do
205:       transition [:opened] => :closed
206:     end
207: 
208:     event :mark_as_merged do
209:       transition [:opened, :locked] => :merged
210:     end
211: 
212:     event :reopen do
213:       transition closed: :opened
214:     end
215: 
216:     event :lock_mr do
217:       transition [:opened] => :locked
218:     end
219: 
220:     event :unlock_mr do
221:       transition locked: :opened
222:     end
223: 
224:     before_transition any => [:opened, :merged] do |merge_request|
225:       merge_request.merge_jid = nil
226:     end
227: 
228:     before_transition any => :closed do |merge_request|
229:       merge_request.merge_error = nil
230:     end
231: 
232:     before_transition any => :merged do |merge_request|
233:       merge_request.merge_error = nil
234:       merge_request.metrics.first_contribution = true if merge_request.first_contribution?
235:     end
236: 
237:     after_transition any => :opened do |merge_request|
238:       merge_request.run_after_commit do
239:         UpdateHeadPipelineForMergeRequestWorker.perform_async(merge_request.id)
240:       end
241:     end
242: 
243:     state :opened, value: MergeRequest.available_states[:opened]
244:     state :closed, value: MergeRequest.available_states[:closed]
245:     state :merged, value: MergeRequest.available_states[:merged]
246:     state :locked, value: MergeRequest.available_states[:locked]
247:   end
248: 
249:   # NOTE: If you modify this state machine (transitions or callbacks), you must also
250:   # review and update the batch method below (batch_mark_as_unchecked).
251:   # Batch methods bypass callbacks for performance, so any new callbacks may need
252:   # to be handled manually.
253:   #
254:   # See: https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/work_items/546431
255:   state_machine :merge_status, initial: :unchecked do
256:     event :mark_as_preparing do
257:       transition [:unchecked, :can_be_merged] => :preparing
258:     end
259: 
260:     event :mark_as_unchecked do
261:       transition [:preparing, :can_be_merged, :checking] => :unchecked
262:       transition [:cannot_be_merged, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking] => :cannot_be_merged_recheck
263:     end
264: 
265:     event :mark_as_checking do
266:       transition unchecked: :checking
267:       transition cannot_be_merged_recheck: :cannot_be_merged_rechecking
268:     end
269: 
270:     event :mark_as_mergeable do
271:       transition [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :checking, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking] => :can_be_merged
272:     end
273: 
274:     event :mark_as_unmergeable do
275:       transition [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :checking, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking] => :cannot_be_merged
276:     end
277: 
278:     state :preparing
279:     state :unchecked
280:     state :cannot_be_merged_recheck
281:     state :checking
282:     state :cannot_be_merged_rechecking
283:     state :can_be_merged
284:     state :cannot_be_merged
285: 
286:     around_transition do |merge_request, transition, block|
287:       Gitlab::Timeless.timeless(merge_request, &block)
288:     end
289: 
290:     after_transition any => [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :can_be_merged, :cannot_be_merged] do |merge_request, transition|
291:       next if merge_request.skip_merge_status_trigger
292: 
293:       merge_request.run_after_commit do
294:         GraphqlTriggers.merge_request_merge_status_updated(merge_request)
295:       end
296:     end
297: 
298:     after_transition any => :can_be_merged do |merge_request, transition|
299:       merge_request.run_after_commit do
300:         # We find it instead of reloading in order not to clear any other attributes
301:         next unless MergeRequest.where(id: merge_request.id, auto_merge_enabled: true).exists?
302: 
303:         AutoMergeProcessWorker.perform_async({ 'merge_request_id' => merge_request.id })
304:       end
305:     end
306: 
307:     # rubocop: disable CodeReuse/ServiceClass
308:     after_transition [:unchecked, :checking] => :cannot_be_merged do |merge_request, _|
309:       merge_request.run_after_commit do
310:         next unless merge_request.notify_conflict?
311: 
312:         publish_code_conflict_event
313:         NotificationService.new.merge_request_unmergeable(merge_request)
314:         TodoService.new.merge_request_became_unmergeable(merge_request)
315:       end
316:     end
317:     # rubocop: enable CodeReuse/ServiceClass
318: 
319:     def check_state?(merge_status)
320:       [:unchecked, :cannot_be_merged_recheck, :checking, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking].include?(merge_status.to_sym)
321:     end
322: 
323:     # rubocop: disable Style/SymbolProc
324:     before_transition { |merge_request| merge_request.enable_transitioning }
325: 
326:     after_transition { |merge_request| merge_request.disable_transitioning }
327:     # rubocop: enable Style/SymbolProc
328:   end
329: 
330:   # NOTE: Batch transition merge_status to unchecked/cannot_be_merged_recheck.
331:   # This bypasses state machine callbacks for performance but mimics their behavior.
332:   #
333:   # Transitions mirror the `mark_as_unchecked` event defined above.
334:   # Process batch updates in batches as there can be many target merge requests.
335:   def self.batch_mark_as_unchecked(mr_ids, mrs_to_trigger: [], batch_size: 1000)
336:     return if mr_ids.blank?
337: 
338:     mr_ids.each_slice(batch_size) do |batch_ids|
339:       where(id: batch_ids)
340:         .where(merge_status: [:preparing, :can_be_merged, :checking])
341:         .update_all(merge_status: :unchecked)
342: 
343:       where(id: batch_ids)
344:         .where(merge_status: [:cannot_be_merged, :cannot_be_merged_rechecking])
345:         .update_all(merge_status: :cannot_be_merged_recheck)
346:     end
347: 
348:     mrs_to_trigger.each do |mr|
349:       GraphqlTriggers.merge_request_merge_status_updated(mr.reset)
350:     end
351:   end
352: 
353:   # NOTE: Batch transition merge_status to checking/cannot_be_merged_rechecking.
354:   # This bypasses state machine callbacks for performance but mimics their behavior.
355:   #
356:   # Transitions mirror the `mark_as_checking` event defined above.
357:   def self.batch_mark_as_checking(mr_ids, batch_size: 1000)
358:     return if mr_ids.blank?
359: 
360:     mr_ids.each_slice(batch_size) do |batch_ids|
361:       where(id: batch_ids)
362:         .where(merge_status: :unchecked)
363:         .update_all(merge_status: :checking)
364: 
365:       where(id: batch_ids)
366:         .where(merge_status: :cannot_be_merged_recheck)
367:         .update_all(merge_status: :cannot_be_merged_rechecking)
368:     end
369:   end
370: 
371:   def self.batch_clear_merge_error(mr_ids, batch_size: 1000)
372:     return if mr_ids.blank?
373: 
374:     mr_ids.each_slice(batch_size) do |batch_ids|
375:       where(id: batch_ids)
376:         .where.not(merge_error: nil)
377:         .update_all(merge_error: nil)
378:     end
379:   end

381:   # Returns current merge_status except it returns `cannot_be_merged_rechecking` as `checking`
382:   # to avoid exposing unnecessary internal state
383:   def public_merge_status
384:     cannot_be_merged_rechecking? || preparing? ? 'checking' : merge_status
385:   end

762:   def self.order_by_metric_column(metric, direction)
763:     column_expression = MergeRequest::Metrics.arel_table[metric]
764:     column_expression_with_direction = direction == 'ASC' ? column_expression.asc : column_expression.desc
765: 
766:     Gitlab::Pagination::Keyset::Order.build(
767:       [
768:         Gitlab::Pagination::Keyset::ColumnOrderDefinition.new(
769:           attribute_name: "merge_request_metrics_#{metric}",
770:           column_expression: column_expression,
771:           order_expression: column_expression_with_direction.nulls_last,
772:           reversed_order_expression: column_expression_with_direction.reverse.nulls_first,
773:           order_direction: direction,
774:           nullable: :nulls_last,
775:           add_to_projections: true
776:         ),
777:         Gitlab::Pagination::Keyset::ColumnOrderDefinition.new(
778:           attribute_name: 'merge_request_metrics_id',
779:           order_expression: MergeRequest::Metrics.arel_table[:id].desc,
780:           add_to_projections: true
781:         )
782:       ])
783:   end

785:   # Returns the top 100 target branches
786:   #
787:   # The returned value is a Array containing branch names
788:   # sort by updated_at of merge request:
789:   #
790:   #     ['master', 'develop', 'production']
791:   #
792:   # limit - The maximum number of target branch to return.
793:   def self.recent_target_branches(limit: 100)
794:     group(:target_branch)
795:       .select(:target_branch)
796:       .reorder(arel_table[:updated_at].maximum.desc)
797:       .limit(limit)
798:       .pluck(:target_branch)
799:   end

802:     group(:source_branch)
803:       .select(:source_branch)
804:       .reorder(arel_table[:updated_at].maximum.desc)
805:       .limit(limit)
806:       .pluck(:source_branch)
807:   end

879:   # Returns all the merge requests from an ActiveRecord:Relation.
880:   #
881:   # This method uses a UNION as it usually operates on the result of
882:   # ProjectsFinder#execute. PostgreSQL in particular doesn't always like queries
883:   # using multiple sub-queries especially when combined with an OR statement.
884:   # UNIONs on the other hand perform much better in these cases.
885:   #
886:   # relation - An ActiveRecord::Relation that returns a list of Projects.
887:   #
888:   # Returns an ActiveRecord::Relation.
889:   def self.in_projects(relation)
890:     # unscoping unnecessary conditions that'll be applied
891:     # when executing `where("merge_requests.id IN (#{union.to_sql})")`
892:     source = unscoped.where(source_project_id: relation)
893:     target = unscoped.where(target_project_id: relation)
894: 
895:     from_union([source, target])
896:   end
897: 
898:   # This is used after project import, to reset the IDs to the correct
899:   # values. It is not intended to be called without having already scoped the
900:   # relation.
901:   #
902:   # Only set `regular` merge request diffs as latest so `merge_head` diff
903:   # won't be considered as `MergeRequest#merge_request_diff`.
904:   def self.set_latest_merge_request_diff_ids!
905:     update = "
906:       latest_merge_request_diff_id = (
907:         SELECT MAX(id)
908:         FROM merge_request_diffs
909:         WHERE merge_requests.id = merge_request_diffs.merge_request_id
910:         AND merge_request_diffs.diff_type = #{MergeRequestDiff.diff_types[:regular]}
911:       )".squish
912: 
913:     self.each_batch do |batch|
914:       batch.update_all(update)
915:     end
916:   end

1075:   # Calls `MergeWorker` to proceed with the merge process and
1076:   # updates `merge_jid` with the MergeWorker#jid.
1077:   # This helps tracking enqueued and ongoing merge jobs.
1078:   def merge_async(user_id, params)
1079:     jid = MergeWorker.with_status.perform_async(id, user_id, params.to_h)
1080:     update_merge_jid(jid)
1081: 
1082:     # merge_ongoing? depends on merge_jid
1083:     # expire etag cache since the attribute is changed without triggering callbacks
1084:     expire_etag_cache
1085:   end
1086: 
1087:   # Set off a rebase asynchronously, atomically updating the `rebase_jid` of
1088:   # the MR so that the status of the operation can be tracked.
1089:   def rebase_async(user_id, skip_ci: false)
1090:     with_rebase_lock do
1091:       raise ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError if !open? || rebase_in_progress?
1092: 
1093:       # Although there is a race between setting rebase_jid here and clearing it
1094:       # in the RebaseWorker, it can't do any harm since we check both that the
1095:       # attribute is set *and* that the sidekiq job is still running. So a JID
1096:       # for a completed RebaseWorker is equivalent to a nil JID.
1097:       jid = Sidekiq::Worker.skipping_transaction_check do
1098:         RebaseWorker.with_status.perform_async(id, user_id, skip_ci)
1099:       end
1100: 
1101:       update_column(:rebase_jid, jid)
1102:     end
1103: 
1104:     # rebase_in_progress? depends on rebase_jid
1105:     # expire etag cache since the attribute is changed without triggering callbacks
1106:     expire_etag_cache
1107:   end

1365:   def validate_branches
1366:     return unless target_project && source_project
1367: 
1368:     if target_project == source_project && target_branch == source_branch
1369:       errors.add :branch_conflict, "You can't use same project/branch for source and target"
1370:       return
1371:     end
1372: 
1373:     [:source_branch, :target_branch].each { |attr| validate_branch_name(attr) }
1374: 
1375:     if opened?
1376:       conflicting_mr = existing_mrs_targeting_same_branch.first
1377: 
1378:       if conflicting_mr
1379:         errors.add(
1380:           :validate_branches,
1381:           conflicting_mr_message(conflicting_mr)
1382:         )
1383:       end
1384:     end

1387:   def conflicting_mr_message(conflicting_mr)
1388:     format(

1390:       conflicting_mr_reference: conflicting_mr.to_reference
1391:     )
1392:   end

1396:

1398: 
1399:     return unless branch
1400: 
1401:     errors.add(attr) unless Gitlab::GitRefValidator.validate_merge_request_branch(branch)
1402:   end

1423:     return true unless target_project && source_project
1424:     return true if target_project == source_project
1425:     return true unless source_project_missing?

1438:     # While the MergeRequest is locked, it should present itself as 'merge ongoing'.
1439:     # The unlocking process is handled by StuckMergeJobsWorker scheduled in Cron.

1843:   # If the merge request closes any issues, save this information in the
1844:   # `MergeRequestsClosingIssues` model. This is a performance optimization.
1845:   # Calculating this information for a number of merge requests requires
1846:   # running `ReferenceExtractor` on each of them separately.
1847:   # This optimization does not apply to issues from external sources.
1848:   def cache_merge_request_closes_issues!(current_user = self.author)
1849:     return if closed? || merged?
1850: 
1851:     issues_to_close_ids = closes_issues(current_user).reject { |issue| issue.is_a?(ExternalIssue) }.map(&:id)
1852: 
1853:     transaction do
1854:       merge_request_closing_issues.from_mr_description.delete_all
1855: 
1856:       updated_issue_ids = update_cached_closing_issues_from_description!(issues_to_close_ids)
1857:       issue_ids_to_create = issues_to_close_ids - updated_issue_ids
1858:       next unless issue_ids_to_create.any?
1859: 
1860:       bulk_insert_cached_closing_issues(issue_ids_to_create)
1861:     end

1974:   def default_merge_commit_message(include_description: false, user: nil)
1975:     if self.target_project.merge_commit_template.present? && !include_description
1976:       return ::Gitlab::MergeRequests::MessageGenerator.new(merge_request: self, current_user: user).merge_commit_message
1977:     end
1978: 
1979:     closes_issues_references = visible_closing_issues_for.map do |issue|
1980:       issue.to_reference(target_project)
1981:     end
1982: 
1983:     message = [
1984:       "Merge branch '#{source_branch}' into '#{target_branch}'",
1985:       title
1986:     ]
1987: 
1988:     if !include_description && closes_issues_references.present?
1989:       message << "Closes #{closes_issues_references.to_sentence}"
1990:     end
1991: 
1992:     message << description if include_description && description.present?
1993:     message << "See merge request #{to_reference(full: true)}"
1994: 
1995:     message.join("\n\n")
1996:   end

2403:   # Exists only for merged merge requests
2404:   def commit_to_revert
2405:     return unless merged?
2406: 
2407:     # By default, it's equal to a merge commit
2408:     return merge_commit if merge_commit
2409: 
2410:     # But in case of fast-forward merge merge commits are not created
2411:     # To solve that we can use `squash_commit` if the merge request was squashed
2412:     return squash_commit if squash_commit
2413: 
2414:     # Edge case: one commit in the merge request without merge or squash commit
2415:     return project.commit(diff_head_sha) if commits_count == 1
2416: 
2417:     nil
2418:   end

2420:   def commit_to_cherry_pick

2424:   def can_be_reverted?(current_user)
2425:     return false unless merged_at
2426: 
2427:     commit = commit_to_revert
2428:     return false unless commit
2429: 
2430:     # It is not guaranteed that Note#created_at will be strictly later than
2431:     # MergeRequestMetric#merged_at. Nanoseconds on MySQL may break this
2432:     # comparison, as will a HA environment if clocks are not *precisely*
2433:     # synchronized. Add a minute's leeway to compensate for both possibilities
2434:     cutoff = merged_at - 1.minute
2435: 
2436:     notes_association = notes_with_associations.where('created_at >= ?', cutoff)
2437: 
2438:     !commit.has_been_reverted?(current_user, notes_association)
2439:   end

2441:   def merged_at
2442:     strong_memoize(:merged_at) do
2443:       next unless merged?
2444: 
2445:       metrics&.merged_at ||
2446:         merge_event&.created_at ||
2447:         resource_state_events.find_by(state: :merged)&.created_at ||
2448:         notes.system.reorder(nil).find_by(note: 'merged')&.created_at
2449:     end
2450:   end

2460:   # rubocop: disable CodeReuse/ServiceClass
2461:   def update_diff_discussion_positions(old_diff_refs:, new_diff_refs:, current_user: nil)
2462:     return unless has_complete_diff_refs?
2463:     return if new_diff_refs == old_diff_refs
2464: 
2465:     active_diff_discussions = self.notes.new_diff_notes.discussions.select do |discussion|
2466:       discussion.active?(old_diff_refs)
2467:     end
2468:     return if active_diff_discussions.empty?
2469: 
2470:     paths = active_diff_discussions.flat_map { |n| n.diff_file.paths }.uniq
2471: 
2472:     active_discussions_resolved = active_diff_discussions.all?(&:resolved?)
2473: 
2474:     service = Discussions::UpdateDiffPositionService.new(
2475:       self.project,
2476:       current_user,
2477:       old_diff_refs: old_diff_refs,
2478:       new_diff_refs: new_diff_refs,
2479:       paths: paths
2480:     )
2481: 
2482:     active_diff_discussions.each do |discussion|
2483:       service.execute(discussion)
2484:       discussion.clear_memoized_values
2485:     end
2486: 
2487:     # If they were all already resolved, this method will have already been called.
2488:     # If they all don't get resolved, we don't need to call the method
2489:     # If they go from unresolved -> resolved, then we call the method
2490:     if !active_discussions_resolved &&

3146:   def with_rebase_lock
3147:     with_retried_nowait_lock { yield }
3148:   end
3149: 
3150:   # If the merge request is idle in transaction or has a SELECT FOR
3151:   # UPDATE, we don't want to block indefinitely or this could cause a
3152:   # queue of SELECT FOR UPDATE calls. Instead, try to get the lock for
3153:   # 5 s before raising an error to the user.
3154:   def with_retried_nowait_lock
3155:     # Try at most 0.25 + (1.5 * .25) + (1.5^2 * .25) ... (1.5^5 * .25) = 5.2 s to get the lock
3156:     Retriable.retriable(on: ActiveRecord::LockWaitTimeout, tries: 6, base_interval: 0.25) do
3157:       with_lock('FOR UPDATE NOWAIT') do
3158:         yield
3159:       end
3160:     end
3161:   rescue ActiveRecord::LockWaitTimeout => e
3162:     Gitlab::ErrorTracking.track_exception(e)
3163:     raise RebaseLockTimeout, _('Failed to enqueue the rebase operation, possibly due to a long-lived transaction. Try again later.')
3164:   end

<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: # == Issuable concern
4: #
5: # Contains common functionality shared between Issues and MergeRequests
6: #
7: # Used by Issue, MergeRequest, Epic
8: #
9: module Issuable
10:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
11:   include Gitlab::SQL::Pattern
12:   include Redactable
13:   include CacheMarkdownField
14:   include Participable
15:   include Mentionable
16:   include Milestoneable
17:   include Subscribable
18:   include StripAttribute
19:   include Awardable
20:   include Taskable
21:   include Importable
22:   include Transitionable
23:   include Editable
24:   include AfterCommitQueue
25:   include Sortable
26:   include CreatedAtFilterable
27:   include UpdatedAtFilterable
28:   include ClosedAtFilterable
29:   include VersionedDescription
30:   include SortableTitle
31:   include Exportable
32:   include ReportableChanges
33:   include Import::HasImportSource
34: 
35:   TITLE_LENGTH_MAX = 255
36:   SEARCHABLE_FIELDS = %w[title description].freeze
37:   MAX_NUMBER_OF_ASSIGNEES_OR_REVIEWERS = 200
38: 
39:   STATE_ID_MAP = {
40:     opened: 1,
41:     closed: 2,
42:     merged: 3,
43:     locked: 4
44:   }.with_indifferent_access.freeze
45: 
46:   included do
47:     cache_markdown_field :title, pipeline: :single_line
48:     cache_markdown_field :description, issuable_reference_expansion_enabled: true
49: 
50:     redact_field :description
51: 
52:     belongs_to :author, class_name: 'User'
53:     belongs_to :updated_by, class_name: 'User'
54:     belongs_to :last_edited_by, class_name: 'User'
55: 
56:     has_many :notes, -> { extending Notes::ParticipantAssociationsExtension }, as: :noteable, inverse_of: :noteable, dependent: :destroy # rubocop:disable Cop/ActiveRecordDependent
57:     has_many :notes_with_possible_mentions, -> {
58:       Note.from_union(
59:         [
60:           user,
61:           system.with_possible_mentions
62:         ], remove_duplicates: false
63:       ).extending(Notes::ParticipantAssociationsExtension)
64:     }, class_name: 'Note', as: :noteable, inverse_of: :noteable
65: 
66:     has_many :note_authors, -> { distinct }, through: :notes, source: :author
67:     has_many :user_note_authors, -> { distinct.where("notes.system = false") }, through: :notes, source: :author
68: 
69:     has_many :label_links, as: :target, inverse_of: :target
70:     has_many :labels, through: :label_links
71:     has_many :todos, as: :target
72: 
73:     delegate :name,
74:       :email,
75:       :public_email,
76:       to: :author,
77:       allow_nil: true,
78:       prefix: true
79: 
80:     validates :author, presence: true
81:     validates :title, presence: true, length: { maximum: TITLE_LENGTH_MAX }
82:     # we validate the description against the limit only for Issuables being created and on updates if
83:     # the description changes to avoid breaking the existing Issuables which may have their descriptions longer
84:     validates :description, bytesize: { maximum: -> { Gitlab::CurrentSettings.description_and_note_max_size } },
85:       if: :validate_description_length?
86:     validate :validate_assignee_size_length, unless: :importing?
87: 
88:     before_validation :truncate_description_on_import!
89: 
90:     scope :authored, ->(user) { where(author_id: user) }
91:     scope :not_authored, ->(user) { where.not(author_id: user) }
92:     scope :recent, -> { reorder(id: :desc) }
93:     scope :of_projects, ->(ids) { where(project_id: ids) }
94:     scope :with_state, ->(*states) { where(state_id: states.flatten.map { |s| STATE_ID_MAP[s] }) }
95:     scope :opened, -> { with_state(:opened) }
96:     scope :closed, -> { with_state(:closed) }
97: 
98:     # rubocop:disable GitlabSecurity/SqlInjection
99:     # The `assignee_association_name` method is not an user input.
100:     scope :assigned, -> do
101:       where("EXISTS (SELECT TRUE FROM #{assignee_association_name}_assignees \
102:         WHERE #{assignee_association_name}_id = #{assignee_association_name}s.id)")
103:     end
104:     scope :unassigned, -> do
105:       where("NOT EXISTS (SELECT TRUE FROM #{assignee_association_name}_assignees \
106:         WHERE #{assignee_association_name}_id = #{assignee_association_name}s.id)")
107:     end
108:     scope :assigned_to, ->(users) do
109:       assignees_class = reflect_on_association("#{assignee_association_name}_assignees").klass
110: 
111:       condition = assignees_class.where(user_id: users)
112:         .where(Arel.sql("#{assignee_association_name}_id = #{assignee_association_name}s.id"))
113:       where(condition.arel.exists)
114:     end
115:     scope :not_assigned_to, ->(users) do
116:       assignees_class = reflect_on_association("#{assignee_association_name}_assignees").klass
117: 
118:       condition = assignees_class.where(user_id: users)
119:         .where(Arel.sql("#{assignee_association_name}_id = #{assignee_association_name}s.id"))
120:       where(condition.arel.exists.not)
121:     end
122:     # rubocop:enable GitlabSecurity/SqlInjection
123: 
124:     scope :without_label, -> {
125:       joins("LEFT OUTER JOIN label_links ON label_links.target_type = '#{name}' \
126:       AND label_links.target_id = #{table_name}.id").where(label_links: { id: nil })
127:     }
128:     scope :with_label_ids, ->(label_ids) { joins(:label_links).where(label_links: { label_id: label_ids }) }
129:     scope :join_project, -> { joins(:project) }
130:     scope :inc_notes_with_associations, -> { includes(notes: [:project, :author, :award_emoji]) }
131:     scope :references_project, -> { references(:project) }
132:     scope :non_archived, -> { join_project.merge(Project.self_and_ancestors_non_archived) }
133: 
134:     scope :includes_for_bulk_update, -> do
135:       associations = %i[
136:         author assignees epic group labels metrics project source_project target_project
137:       ].select do |association|
138:         reflect_on_association(association)
139:       end
140: 
141:       includes(*associations)
142:     end
143: 
144:     attr_mentionable :title, pipeline: :single_line
145:     attr_mentionable :description
146: 
147:     participant :author
148:     participant :system_note_authors
149:     participant :notes_for_participants
150:     participant :assignees
151: 
152:     strip_attributes! :title
153: 
154:     class << self
155:       def labels_hash
156:         issue_labels = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = [] }
157: 
158:         relation = unscoped.where(id: self.select(:id)).eager_load(:labels)
159:         relation.pluck(:id, 'labels.title').each do |issue_id, label|
160:           issue_labels[issue_id] << label if label.present?

162: 
163:         issue_labels

172:         format(_("total must be less than or equal to %{size}"), size: MAX_NUMBER_OF_ASSIGNEES_OR_REVIEWERS)
173:       end
174:     end

181:     # http://api.rubyonrails.org/classes/ActiveRecord/Locking/Optimistic.html
182:     def locking_enabled?

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Noteable
4:   extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5: 
6:   # This object is used to gather noteable meta data for list displays
7:   # avoiding n+1 queries and improving performance.
8:   NoteableMeta = Struct.new(:user_notes_count)
9: 
10:   MAX_NOTES_LIMIT = 5_000
11: 
12:   class_methods do
13:     # `Noteable` class names that support replying to individual notes.
14:     def replyable_types
15:       %w[Issue MergeRequest AbuseReport WikiPage::Meta]
16:     end
17: 
18:     # `Noteable` class names that support resolvable notes.
19:     def resolvable_types
20:       %w[Issue MergeRequest DesignManagement::Design AbuseReport WikiPage::Meta]
21:     end
22: 
23:     # `Noteable` class names that support creating/forwarding individual notes.
24:     def email_creatable_types
25:       %w[Issue]
26:     end
27:   end
28: 
29:   # The timestamp of the note (e.g. the :created_at or :updated_at attribute if provided via
30:   # API call)
31:   def system_note_timestamp
32:     @system_note_timestamp || Time.current # rubocop:disable Gitlab/ModuleWithInstanceVariables
33:   end
34: 
35:   attr_writer :system_note_timestamp
36: 
37:   def base_class_name
38:     self.class.base_class.name
39:   end
40: 
41:   # Convert this Noteable class name to a format usable by notifications.
42:   #
43:   # Examples:
44:   #
45:   #   noteable.class           # => MergeRequest
46:   #   noteable.human_class_name # => "merge request"
47:   def human_class_name
48:     @human_class_name ||= base_class_name.titleize.downcase
49:   end
50: 
51:   def supports_resolvable_notes?
52:     self.class.resolvable_types.include?(base_class_name)
53:   end
54: 
55:   def supports_discussions?
56:     DiscussionNote.noteable_types.include?(base_class_name)
57:   end
58: 
59:   def supports_replying_to_individual_notes?
60:     supports_discussions? && self.class.replyable_types.include?(base_class_name)
61:   end
62: 
63:   def supports_replying_to_system_notes?
64:     false
65:   end
66: 
67:   def supports_creating_notes_by_email?
68:     self.class.email_creatable_types.include?(base_class_name)
69:   end
70: 
71:   def supports_suggestion?
72:     false
73:   end
74: 
75:   def discussions_rendered_on_frontend?
76:     false
77:   end
78: 
79:   def preloads_discussion_diff_highlighting?
80:     false
81:   end
82: 
83:   def has_any_diff_note_positions?
84:     notes.any? && DiffNotePosition.where(note: notes).exists?
85:   end
86: 
87:   def discussion_notes
88:     notes
89:   end
90: 
91:   delegate :find_discussion, to: :discussion_notes
92: 
93:   def discussions
94:     @discussions ||= discussion_notes
95:       .inc_relations_for_view(self)
96:       .discussions(self)
97:   end
98: 
99:   def discussion_ids_relation
100:     notes.select(:discussion_id)
101:       .group(:discussion_id)
102:       .order('MIN(created_at), MIN(id)')
103:   end
104: 
105:   # This does not consider OutOfContextDiscussions in MRs
106:   # where notes from commits are overridden so that they have
107:   # the same discussion_id
108:   def discussion_root_note_ids(notes_filter:, sort: :created_asc)
109:     sort = :created_asc if sort.nil?
110:     relations = []
111: 
112:     relations << discussion_notes.select(
113:       "'notes' AS table_name",
114:       'MIN(id) AS id',
115:       'MIN(created_at) AS created_at',
116:       'ARRAY_AGG(id) AS ids'
117:     ).with_notes_filter(notes_filter)
118:      .group(:discussion_id)
119: 
120:     if notes_filter != UserPreference::NOTES_FILTERS[:only_comments]
121:       relations += synthetic_note_ids_relations
122:     end
123: 
124:     discussions = Note.from_union(relations, remove_duplicates: false)
125:                     .select(:table_name, :id, :created_at, :ids)
126: 
127:     case sort
128:     when :created_asc
129:       discussions.order_created_at_id_asc
130:     when :created_desc
131:       discussions.order_created_at_id_desc
132:     else
133:       raise ArgumentError, 'Invalid sort order'
134:     end
135:   end
136: 
137:   def capped_notes_count(max)
138:     notes.limit(max).count
139:   end
140: 
141:   def grouped_diff_discussions(...)
142:     # Doesn't use `discussion_notes`, because this may include commit diff notes
143:     # besides MR diff notes, that we do not want to display on the MR Changes tab.
144:     notes.inc_relations_for_view(self).grouped_diff_discussions(...)
145:   end
146: 
147:   # rubocop:disable Gitlab/ModuleWithInstanceVariables
148:   def resolvable_discussions
149:     @resolvable_discussions ||=
150:       if defined?(@discussions)
151:         @discussions.select(&:resolvable?)
152:       else
153:         discussion_notes.resolvable.discussions(self)
154:       end
155:   end
156:   # rubocop:enable Gitlab/ModuleWithInstanceVariables
157: 
158:   def discussions_resolvable?
159:     resolvable_discussions.any?(&:resolvable?)
160:   end
161: 
162:   def discussions_resolved?
163:     discussions_resolvable? && resolvable_discussions.none?(&:to_be_resolved?)
164:   end
165: 
166:   def discussions_to_be_resolved
167:     @discussions_to_be_resolved ||= resolvable_discussions.select(&:to_be_resolved?)
168:   end
169: 
170:   def discussions_can_be_resolved_by?(user)
171:     discussions_to_be_resolved.all? { |discussion| discussion.can_resolve?(user) }
172:   end
173: 
174:   def lockable?
175:     [MergeRequest, Issue].include?(self.class)
176:   end
177: 
178:   def real_time_notes_enabled?
179:     false
180:   end
181: 
182:   def broadcast_notes_changed
183:     return unless discussions_rendered_on_frontend?
184:     return unless real_time_notes_enabled?
185: 
186:     Noteable::NotesChannel.broadcast_to(self, event: 'updated')
187:   end
188: 
189:   def after_note_created(_note)
190:     # no-op
191:   end
192: 
193:   def after_note_destroyed(_note)
194:     # no-op
195:   end
196: 
197:   # Email address that an authorized user can send/forward an email to be added directly
198:   # to an issue or merge request.
199:   # example: incoming+h5bp-html5-boilerplate-8-1234567890abcdef123456789-issue-34@localhost.com
200:   def creatable_note_email_address(author)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class UpdateService < MergeRequests::BaseService
5:     include Gitlab::InternalEventsTracking
6:     extend ::Gitlab::Utils::Override
7: 
8:     def initialize(project:, current_user: nil, params: {})
9:       super
10: 
11:       @target_branch_was_deleted = @params.delete(:target_branch_was_deleted)
12:     end
13: 
14:     def execute(merge_request)
15:       if Gitlab::Utils.to_boolean(params[:draft])
16:         merge_request.title = merge_request.draft_title
17:       end
18: 
19:       if params.key?(:merge_after)
20:         merge_after = params.delete(:merge_after)
21:         UpdateMergeScheduleService.new(merge_request, merge_after: merge_after).execute
22:       end
23: 
24:       update_merge_request_with_specialized_service(merge_request) || general_fallback(merge_request)
25:     end
26: 
27:     def handle_changes(merge_request, options)
28:       super
29:       old_associations = options.fetch(:old_associations, {})
30:       old_labels = old_associations.fetch(:labels, [])
31:       old_mentioned_users = old_associations.fetch(:mentioned_users, [])
32:       old_assignees = old_associations.fetch(:assignees, [])
33:       old_reviewers = old_associations.fetch(:reviewers, [])
34:       old_timelogs = old_associations.fetch(:timelogs, [])
35:       changed_fields = merge_request.previous_changes.keys
36: 
37:       resolve_todos(merge_request, old_labels, old_assignees, old_reviewers)
38: 
39:       if merge_request.previous_changes.include?('title') ||
40:           merge_request.previous_changes.include?('description')
41:         todo_service.update_merge_request(merge_request, current_user, old_mentioned_users)
42:         handle_title_and_desc_edits(merge_request, merge_request.previous_changes.keys)
43:       end
44: 
45:       handle_target_branch_change(merge_request, changed_fields)
46:       handle_draft_status_change(merge_request, changed_fields)
47: 
48:       track_title_and_desc_edits(changed_fields)
49:       track_discussion_lock_toggle(merge_request, changed_fields)
50:       track_time_estimate_and_spend_edits(merge_request, old_timelogs, changed_fields)
51:       track_labels_change(merge_request, old_labels)
52: 
53:       notify_if_labels_added(merge_request, old_labels)
54:       notify_if_mentions_added(merge_request, old_mentioned_users)
55: 
56:       # Since #mark_as_unchecked triggers an update action through the MR's
57:       #   state machine, we want to push this as far down in the process so we
58:       #   avoid resetting #ActiveModel::Dirty
59:       #
60:       if changed_fields.include?('target_branch') ||
61:           changed_fields.include?('source_branch')
62:         merge_request.mark_as_unchecked unless merge_request.unchecked?
63: 
64:         # Delete HEAD diff and re-enqueue mergeability check so we get to generate
65:         # a fresh HEAD diff when either branches have been changed.
66:         merge_request.merge_head_diff&.destroy!
67:         merge_request.check_mergeability(async: true)
68:       end
69:     end
70: 
71:     def handle_task_changes(merge_request)
72:       todo_service.resolve_todos_for_target(merge_request, current_user)
73:       todo_service.update_merge_request(merge_request, current_user)
74:     end
75: 
76:     def after_update(merge_request, old_associations)
77:       super
78: 
79:       merge_request.cache_merge_request_closes_issues!(current_user) unless merge_request.auto_merge_enabled?
80:       @trigger_work_item_updated = true
81:     end
82: 
83:     private
84: 
85:     attr_reader :target_branch_was_deleted
86: 
87:     def trigger_updated_work_item_on_closing_issues(merge_request, old_closing_issues_ids)
88:       new_issue_ids = merge_request.merge_request_closing_issues.limit(1000).pluck(:issue_id) # rubocop:disable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord -- Implementation would be the same in the model
89:       all_issue_ids = new_issue_ids | old_closing_issues_ids
90:       return if all_issue_ids.blank?
91: 
92:       WorkItem.id_in(all_issue_ids).find_each(batch_size: 100) do |work_item| # rubocop:disable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord -- Implementation would be the same in the model
93:         GraphqlTriggers.work_item_updated(work_item)
94:       end
95:     end
96: 
97:     override :associations_before_update
98:     def associations_before_update(merge_request)
99:       super.merge(
100:         closing_issues_ids: merge_request.merge_request_closing_issues.limit(1000).pluck(:issue_id) # rubocop:disable CodeReuse/ActiveRecord -- Implementation would be the same in the model
101:       )
102:     end
103: 
104:     override :change_state
105:     def change_state(merge_request, state_event)
106:       return unless super
107: 
108:       @trigger_work_item_updated = true
109:     end
110: 
111:     override :trigger_update_subscriptions
112:     def trigger_update_subscriptions(merge_request, old_associations)
113:       return unless @trigger_work_item_updated
114: 
115:       trigger_updated_work_item_on_closing_issues(
116:         merge_request,
117:         old_associations.fetch(:closing_issues_ids, [])
118:       )
119:     end
120: 
121:     def general_fallback(merge_request)
122:       # We don't allow change of source/target projects and source branch
123:       # after merge request was created
124:       params.delete(:source_project_id)
125:       params.delete(:target_project_id)
126:       params.delete(:source_branch)
127: 
128:       if merge_request.closed_or_merged_without_fork?
129:         params.delete(:target_branch)
130:         params.delete(:force_remove_source_branch)
131:       end
132: 
133:       update_task_event(merge_request) || update(merge_request)
134:     end
135: 
136:     def handle_title_and_desc_edits(merge_request, changed_fields)
137:       fields = %w[title description]
138: 
139:       return unless changed_fields.any? { |field| fields.include?(field) }
140:       return unless merge_request.auto_merge_enabled?
141:       return unless should_publish_update_event?(merge_request, changed_fields)
142: 
143:       ::Gitlab::EventStore.publish(
144:         ::MergeRequests::AutoMerge::TitleDescriptionUpdateEvent.new(data: { current_user_id: current_user.id, merge_request_id: merge_request.id })
145:       )
146:     end
147: 
148:     def should_publish_update_event?(merge_request, changed_fields)
149:       changed_fields.include?('title') &&
150:         merge_request.project.merge_request_title_regex.present?
151:     end
152: 
153:     def track_title_and_desc_edits(changed_fields)
154:       tracked_fields = %w[title description]
155: 
156:       return unless changed_fields.any? { |field| tracked_fields.include?(field) }
157: 
158:       tracked_fields.each do |action|
159:         next unless changed_fields.include?(action)
160: 
161:         merge_request_activity_counter
162:           .public_send(:"track_#{action}_edit_action", user: current_user) # rubocop:disable GitlabSecurity/PublicSend
163:       end
164:     end
165: 
166:     def track_discussion_lock_toggle(merge_request, changed_fields)
167:       return unless changed_fields.include?('discussion_locked')
168: 
169:       if merge_request.discussion_locked
170:         merge_request_activity_counter.track_discussion_locked_action(user: current_user)
171:       else
172:         merge_request_activity_counter.track_discussion_unlocked_action(user: current_user)
173:       end
174:     end
175: 
176:     def track_time_estimate_and_spend_edits(merge_request, old_timelogs, changed_fields)
177:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_time_estimate_changed_action(user: current_user) if changed_fields.include?('time_estimate')
178:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_time_spent_changed_action(user: current_user) if old_timelogs != merge_request.timelogs
179:     end
180: 
181:     def track_labels_change(merge_request, old_labels)
182:       return if Set.new(merge_request.labels) == Set.new(old_labels)
183: 
184:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_labels_changed_action(user: current_user)
185:     end
186: 
187:     def notify_if_labels_added(merge_request, old_labels)
188:       added_labels = merge_request.labels - old_labels
189: 
190:       return unless added_labels.present?
191: 
192:       notification_service.async.relabeled_merge_request(
193:         merge_request,
194:         added_labels,
195:         current_user
196:       )
197:     end
198: 
199:     def notify_if_mentions_added(merge_request, old_mentioned_users)
200:       added_mentions = merge_request.mentioned_users(current_user) - old_mentioned_users
201: 
202:       return unless added_mentions.present?
203: 
204:       notification_service.async.new_mentions_in_merge_request(
205:         merge_request,
206:         added_mentions,
207:         current_user
208:       )
209:     end
210: 
211:     def resolve_todos(merge_request, old_labels, old_assignees, old_reviewers)
212:       return unless has_changes?(merge_request, old_labels: old_labels, old_assignees: old_assignees, old_reviewers: old_reviewers)
213: 
214:       resolve_todos_for(merge_request)
215:     end
216: 
217:     def handle_target_branch_change(merge_request, changes)
218:       return unless changes.include?('target_branch')
219: 
220:       create_branch_change_note(
221:         merge_request,
222:         'target',
223:         target_branch_was_deleted ? 'delete' : 'update',
224:         merge_request.previous_changes['target_branch'].first,
225:         merge_request.target_branch
226:       )
227: 
228:       delete_approvals_on_target_branch_change(merge_request)
229: 
230:       # `target_branch_was_deleted` is set to true when MR gets re-targeted due to
231:       # deleted target branch. In this case we don't want to create a new pipeline
232:       # on behalf of MR author.
233:       # We nullify head_pipeline_id to force that a new pipeline is explicitly
234:       # created in order to pass mergeability checks.
235:       if target_branch_was_deleted
236:         merge_request.head_pipeline_id = nil
237:         merge_request.retargeted = true
238: 
239:         track_internal_event(
240:           'retarget_merge_request_on_target_branch_merge',
241:           user: current_user,
242:           project: merge_request.target_project
243:         )
244:       else
245:         refresh_pipelines_on_merge_requests(merge_request, allow_duplicate: true)
246:       end
247: 
248:       abort_auto_merge(merge_request, 'target branch was changed')
249:     end
250: 
251:     def handle_draft_status_change(merge_request, changed_fields)
252:       return unless changed_fields.include?("title")
253: 
254:       old_title, new_title = merge_request.previous_changes["title"]
255:       old_title_draft = MergeRequest.draft?(old_title)
256:       new_title_draft = MergeRequest.draft?(new_title)
257: 
258:       if old_title_draft != new_title_draft
259:         # notify the draft status changed. Added/removed message is handled in the
260:         # email template itself, see `change_in_merge_request_draft_status_email` template.
261:         notify_draft_status_changed(merge_request)
262:         trigger_merge_request_status_updated(merge_request)
263:         publish_draft_change_event(merge_request, new_draft_status: new_title_draft)
264:       end
265: 
266:       if !old_title_draft && new_title_draft
267:         # Marked as Draft
268:         merge_request_activity_counter.track_marked_as_draft_action(user: current_user)
269:       elsif old_title_draft && !new_title_draft
270:         # Unmarked as Draft
271:         merge_request_activity_counter.track_unmarked_as_draft_action(user: current_user)
272:       end
273:     end

276:       Gitlab::EventStore.publish(
277:         MergeRequests::DraftStateChangeEvent.new(
278:           data: {
279:             current_user_id: current_user.id,
280:             merge_request_id: merge_request.id,
281:             new_draft_status: new_draft_status
282:           }
283:         )
284:       )
285:     end

288:       notification_service.async.change_in_merge_request_draft_status(
289:         merge_request,
290:         current_user
291:       )
292:     end

295:       SystemNoteService.change_branch(
296:         issuable, issuable.project, current_user, branch_type, event_type,
297:         old_branch, new_branch)
298:     end

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class MergeRequestPolicy < IssuablePolicy
4:   condition(:can_approve) { can_approve? }
5: 
6:   rule { locked }.policy do
7:     prevent :reopen_merge_request
8:   end
9: 
10:   # Only users who can read the merge request can comment.
11:   # Although :read_merge_request is computed in the policy context,
12:   # it would not be safe to prevent :create_note there, since
13:   # note permissions are shared, and this would apply too broadly.
14:   rule { ~can?(:read_merge_request) }.policy do
15:     prevent :create_note
16:     prevent :accept_merge_request
17:     prevent :mark_note_as_internal
18:   end
19: 
20:   rule { can_approve }.policy do
21:     enable :approve_merge_request
22:   end
23: 
24:   rule { can?(:approve_merge_request) & bot }.policy do
25:     enable :reset_merge_request_approvals
26:   end
27: 
28:   rule { ~anonymous & can?(:read_merge_request) }.policy do
29:     enable :create_todo
30:     enable :update_subscription
31:   end
32: 
33:   rule { hidden & ~admin }.policy do
34:     prevent :read_merge_request
35:     prevent :update_merge_request
36:     prevent :approve_merge_request
37:   end
38: 
39:   condition(:can_merge) { @subject.can_be_merged_by?(@user) }
40: 
41:   rule { can_merge }.policy do
42:     enable :accept_merge_request
43:   end
44: 
45:   rule { can?(:admin_merge_request) }.policy do
46:     enable :set_merge_request_metadata
47:   end
48: 
49:   private
50: 
51:   def can_approve?
52:     can?(:update_merge_request) && is_container_member?
53:   end
54: end
55: 
56: MergeRequestPolicy.prepend_mod_with('MergeRequestPolicy')

(End of file - total 56 lines)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class BaseService < ::IssuableBaseService
5:     extend ::Gitlab::Utils::Override
6:     include MergeRequests::AssignsMergeParams
7:     include MergeRequests::ErrorLogger
8:     include Gitlab::Utils::StrongMemoize
9: 
10:     delegate :repository, to: :project
11: 
12:     def initialize(project:, current_user: nil, params: {})
13:       super(container: project, current_user: current_user, params: params)
14:     end
15: 
16:     def create_note(merge_request, state = merge_request.state)
17:       SystemNoteService.change_status(merge_request, merge_request.target_project, current_user, state, nil)
18:     end
19: 
20:     def hook_data(merge_request, action, old_rev: nil, old_associations: {}, system: false, system_action: nil)
21:       # NOTE: actioned_at represents when the webhook action occurred.
22:       #   Setting it to Time.current to capture when the webhook was fired for now, but we can consider allowing each service
23:       #   to provide relevant timestamps to override this later if more precision is required (e.g., approved_at, merged_at, closed_at).
24:       actioned_at = Time.current
25: 
26:       hook_data = merge_request.to_hook_data(current_user, old_associations: old_associations, action: action, actioned_at: actioned_at)
27: 
28:       if old_rev && !Gitlab::Git.blank_ref?(old_rev)
29:         hook_data[:object_attributes][:oldrev] = old_rev
30:       end
31: 
32:       hook_data[:object_attributes][:system] = system
33:       hook_data[:object_attributes][:system_action] = system_action if system && system_action
34: 
35:       hook_data
36:     end
37: 
38:     def execute_hooks(merge_request, action = 'open', old_rev: nil, old_associations: {}, system: false, system_action: nil)
39:       # NOTE: Due to the async merge request diffs generation, we need to skip this for CreateService and execute it in
40:       #   AfterCreateService instead so that the webhook consumers receive the update when diffs are ready.
41:       return if merge_request.skip_ensure_merge_request_diff
42: 
43:       merge_data = Gitlab::Lazy.new { hook_data(merge_request, action, old_rev: old_rev, old_associations: old_associations, system: system, system_action: system_action) }
44:       merge_request.project.execute_hooks(merge_data, :merge_request_hooks)
45:       merge_request.project.execute_integrations(merge_data, :merge_request_hooks)
46: 
47:       execute_external_hooks(merge_request, merge_data)
48:       execute_group_mention_hooks(merge_request, merge_data) if action == 'open'
49: 
50:       enqueue_jira_connect_messages_for(merge_request)
51:     end
52: 
53:     def execute_external_hooks(merge_request, merge_data)
54:       # Implemented in EE
55:     end
56: 
57:     def execute_group_mention_hooks(merge_request, merge_data)
58:       return unless merge_request.instance_of?(MergeRequest)
59: 
60:       args = {
61:         'mentionable_type' => 'MergeRequest',
62:         'mentionable_id' => merge_request.id,
63:         'hook_data' => merge_data,
64:         'is_confidential' => false
65:       }
66: 
67:       merge_request.run_after_commit_or_now do
68:         Integrations::GroupMentionWorker.perform_async(args)
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     def handle_changes(merge_request, options)
73:       old_associations = options.fetch(:old_associations, {})
74:       old_assignees = old_associations.fetch(:assignees, [])
75:       old_reviewers = old_associations.fetch(:reviewers, [])
76: 
77:       handle_assignees_change(merge_request, old_assignees) if merge_request.assignees != old_assignees
78:       handle_reviewers_change(merge_request, old_reviewers) if merge_request.reviewers != old_reviewers
79:     end
80: 
81:     def handle_assignees_change(merge_request, old_assignees)
82:       MergeRequests::HandleAssigneesChangeService
83:         .new(project: project, current_user: current_user)
84:         .async_execute(merge_request, old_assignees)
85:     end
86: 
87:     def handle_reviewers_change(merge_request, old_reviewers)
88:       affected_reviewers = (old_reviewers + merge_request.reviewers) - (old_reviewers & merge_request.reviewers)
89:       create_reviewer_note(merge_request, old_reviewers)
90:       notification_service.async.changed_reviewer_of_merge_request(merge_request, current_user, old_reviewers)
91:       todo_service.reassigned_reviewable(merge_request, current_user, old_reviewers)
92:       invalidate_cache_counts(merge_request, users: affected_reviewers.compact)
93:       invalidate_cache_counts(merge_request, users: merge_request.assignees)
94: 
95:       new_reviewers = merge_request.reviewers - old_reviewers
96:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_users_review_requested(users: new_reviewers)
97:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_reviewers_changed_action(user: current_user)
98:       trigger_merge_request_reviewers_updated(merge_request)
99: 
100:       set_reviewers_approved(merge_request, new_reviewers) if new_reviewers.any?
101:       set_first_reviewer_assigned_at_metrics(merge_request) if new_reviewers.any?
102:       trigger_user_merge_request_updated(merge_request)
103:       publish_assigned_reviewers_event(merge_request, new_reviewers) if new_reviewers.any?
104:     end
105: 
106:     def cleanup_environments(merge_request)
107:       Environments::StopService.new(merge_request.source_project, current_user)
108:                                .execute_for_merge_request_pipeline(merge_request)
109:     end
110: 
111:     def cancel_review_app_jobs!(merge_request)
112:       environments = merge_request.environments_in_head_pipeline.in_review_folder.available
113:       environments.each { |environment| environment.cancel_deployment_jobs! }
114:     end
115: 
116:     def source_project
117:       @source_project ||= merge_request.source_project
118:     end
119: 
120:     def target_project
121:       @target_project ||= merge_request.target_project
122:     end
123: 
124:     # Don't try to print expensive instance variables.
125:     def inspect
126:       return "#<#{self.class}>" unless respond_to?(:merge_request) && merge_request
127: 
128:       "#<#{self.class} #{merge_request.to_reference(full: true)}>"
129:     end
130: 
131:     def merge_request_activity_counter
132:       Gitlab::UsageDataCounters::MergeRequestActivityUniqueCounter
133:     end
134: 
135:     def deactivate_pages_deployments(merge_request)
136:       Pages::DeactivateMrDeploymentsWorker.perform_async(merge_request.id)
137:     end
138: 
139:     private
140: 
141:     def self.constructor_container_arg(value)
142:       { project: value }
143:     end
144: 
145:     def refresh_pipelines_on_merge_requests(merge_request, allow_duplicate: false)
146:       create_pipeline_for(merge_request, current_user, async: true, allow_duplicate: allow_duplicate)
147:     end
148: 
149:     def enqueue_jira_connect_messages_for(merge_request)
150:       return unless project.jira_subscription_exists?
151: 
152:       if Atlassian::JiraIssueKeyExtractor.has_keys?(merge_request.title, merge_request.description)
153:         JiraConnect::SyncMergeRequestWorker.perform_async(merge_request.id, Atlassian::JiraConnect::Client.generate_update_sequence_id)
154:       end
155:     end
156: 
157:     def create(merge_request)
158:       self.params = assign_allowed_merge_params(merge_request, params)
159: 
160:       super
161:     end
162: 
163:     def set_issuable_author(issuable)
164:       params[:author] ||= Gitlab::Auth::Identity.resolve_composite_identity_actor(current_user)
165: 
166:       super
167:     end
168: 
169:     def update(merge_request)
170:       self.params = assign_allowed_merge_params(merge_request, params)
171: 
172:       super
173:     end
174: 
175:     override :handle_quick_actions
176:     def handle_quick_actions(merge_request)
177:       super
178:       handle_draft_event(merge_request)
179:     end
180: 
181:     def handle_draft_event(merge_request)
182:       if draft_event = params.delete(:wip_event)
183:         # We update the title that is provided in the params or we use the mr title
184:         title = params[:title] || merge_request.title
185:         params[:title] = case draft_event
186:                          when 'draft' then MergeRequest.draft_title(title)
187:                          when 'ready' then MergeRequest.draftless_title(title)
188:                          end
189:       end
190:     end
191: 
192:     def request_duo_code_review(merge_request)
193:       # Overridden in EE
194:     end
195: 
196:     def filter_params(merge_request)
197:       super
198: 
199:       unless merge_request.can_allow_collaboration?(current_user)
200:         params.delete(:allow_collaboration)
201:       end
202: 
203:       filter_reviewer(merge_request)
204:     end
205: 
206:     def filter_reviewer(merge_request)
207:       return if params[:reviewer_ids].blank?
208: 
209:       unless can_admin_issuable?(merge_request)
210:         params.delete(:reviewer_ids)
211: 
212:         return
213:       end
214: 
215:       unless merge_request.allows_multiple_reviewers?
216:         params[:reviewer_ids] = params[:reviewer_ids].first(1)
217:       end
218: 
219:       reviewer_ids = User.id_in(params[:reviewer_ids]).select do |reviewer|
220:         link_composite_identity(reviewer) if reviewer.composite_identity_enforced? && reviewer.service_account?
221: 
222:         user_can_read?(merge_request, reviewer)
223:       end.map(&:id)
224: 
225:       if params[:reviewer_ids].map(&:to_s) == [IssuableFinder::Params::NONE]
226:         params[:reviewer_ids] = []
227:       elsif reviewer_ids.any?
228:         params[:reviewer_ids] = reviewer_ids
229:       else
230:         params.delete(:reviewer_ids)
231:       end
232:     end
233: 
234:     def set_reviewers_approved(merge_request, new_reviewers)
235:       approval_users = merge_request.approvals_for_user_ids(new_reviewers.map(&:id))
236: 
237:       merge_request.merge_request_reviewers_with(approval_users.select(:user_id))
238:         .update_all(state: :approved)
239:     end
240: 
241:     def merge_request_metrics_service(merge_request)
242:       MergeRequestMetricsService.new(merge_request.metrics)
243:     end
244:

246:       SystemNoteService.change_issuable_assignees(
247:         merge_request, merge_request.project, current_user, old_assignees)
248:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class CreateService < MergeRequests::BaseService
5:     def execute
6:       set_projects!
7:       set_default_attributes!
8: 
9:       merge_request = MergeRequest.new
10:       merge_request.target_project = @project
11:       merge_request.source_project = @source_project
12:       merge_request.source_branch = params[:source_branch]
13: 
14:       merge_after = params.delete(:merge_after)
15: 
16:       created_merge_request = create(merge_request)
17: 
18:       UpdateMergeScheduleService.new(created_merge_request, merge_after: merge_after).execute
19: 
20:       created_merge_request
21:     end
22: 
23:     def after_create(issuable)
24:       invalidate_all_users_cache_count(issuable)
25: 
26:       current_user_id = current_user.id
27: 
28:       issuable.run_after_commit do
29:         # Add new items to MergeRequests::AfterCreateService if they can
30:         # be performed in Sidekiq
31:         NewMergeRequestWorker.perform_async(issuable.id, current_user_id)
32:       end
33: 
34:       issuable.mark_as_preparing
35: 
36:       super
37:     end
38: 
39:     # expose issuable create method so it can be called from email
40:     # handler CreateMergeRequestHandler
41:     public :create
42: 
43:     private
44: 
45:     def before_create(merge_request)
46:       # If the fetching of the source branch occurs in an ActiveRecord
47:       # callback (e.g. after_create), a database transaction will be
48:       # open while the Gitaly RPC waits. To avoid an idle in transaction
49:       # timeout, we do this before we attempt to save the merge request.
50: 
51:       merge_request.source_branch_exists?
52:       merge_request.target_branch_exists?
53: 
54:       merge_request.skip_ensure_merge_request_diff = true
55:       merge_request.check_for_spam(user: current_user, action: :create)
56:     end
57: 
58:     def set_projects!
59:       # @project is used to determine whether the user can set the merge request's
60:       # assignee, milestone and labels. Whether they can depends on their
61:       # permissions on the target project.
62:       @source_project = @project
63:       @project = Project.find(params[:target_project_id]) if params[:target_project_id]
64: 
65:       # make sure that source/target project ids are not in
66:       # params so it can't be overridden later when updating attributes
67:       # from params when applying quick actions
68:       params.delete(:source_project_id)
69:       params.delete(:target_project_id)
70: 
71:       unless can?(current_user, :create_merge_request_from, @source_project) &&
72:           can?(current_user, :create_merge_request_in, @project)
73: 
74:         raise Gitlab::Access::AccessDeniedError
75:       end
76:     end
77: 
78:     def set_default_attributes!
79:       set_default_squash! unless params.key?(:squash) || params.key?('squash')
80:       set_default_force_remove_source_branch! if params[:force_remove_source_branch].nil?

83:     def set_default_force_remove_source_branch!
84:       params[:force_remove_source_branch] = @source_project.remove_source_branch_after_merge?
85:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module MergeRequests
4:   class AfterCreateService < MergeRequests::BaseService
5:     def execute(merge_request)
6:       merge_request.ensure_merge_request_diff
7: 
8:       prepare_for_mergeability(merge_request)
9:       prepare_merge_request(merge_request)
10:       mark_merge_request_as_prepared(merge_request)
11: 
12:       logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Executing hooks'))
13:       execute_hooks(merge_request)
14:       logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Executed hooks'))
15:     end
16: 
17:     private
18: 
19:     def prepare_for_mergeability(merge_request)
20:       if async_pipeline_creation?(merge_request)
21:         logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Creating pipeline async'))
22:         create_pipeline_for(merge_request, current_user, async: true)
23:         logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Pipeline creating async'))
24:       else
25:         logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Creating pipeline'))
26:         create_pipeline_for(merge_request, current_user, async: false)
27:         merge_request.update_head_pipeline
28:         logger.info(**log_payload(merge_request, 'Pipeline created'))
29:       end
30: 
31:       check_mergeability(merge_request)
32:     end
33: 
34:     def prepare_merge_request(merge_request)
35:       event_service.open_mr(merge_request, current_user)
36: 
37:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_create_mr_action(user: current_user, merge_request: merge_request)
38:       merge_request_activity_counter.track_mr_including_ci_config(user: current_user, merge_request: merge_request)
39: 
40:       notification_service.new_merge_request(merge_request, current_user)
41:       merge_request.diffs(include_stats: false).write_cache
42:       merge_request.create_cross_references!(current_user)
43:       todo_service.new_merge_request(merge_request, current_user)
44:       merge_request.cache_merge_request_closes_issues!(current_user)
45: 
46:       Gitlab::InternalEvents.track_event(
47:         'create_merge_request',
48:         user: current_user,
49:         project: merge_request.target_project
50:       )
51: 
52:       link_lfs_objects(merge_request)
53:     end
54: 
55:     def link_lfs_objects(merge_request)
56:       LinkLfsObjectsService.new(project: merge_request.target_project).execute(merge_request)
57:     end
58: 
59:     def check_mergeability(merge_request)
60:       return unless merge_request.preparing?
61: 
62:       # Need to set to unchecked to be able to check for mergeability or else
63:       # it'll be a no-op.
64:       merge_request.mark_as_unchecked
65:       merge_request.check_mergeability(async: true)
66:     end
67: 
68:     def mark_merge_request_as_prepared(merge_request)
69:       merge_request.update!(prepared_at: Time.current)
70:       GraphqlTriggers.merge_request_merge_status_updated(merge_request)
71:     end
72: 
73:     def logger
74:       @logger ||= Gitlab::AppLogger
75:     end
76: 
77:     def log_payload(merge_request, message)
78:       Gitlab::ApplicationContext.current.merge(
79:         merge_request_id: merge_request.id,
80:         message: message
81:       )
82:     end
83: 
84:     def async_pipeline_creation?(merge_request)
85:       Feature.enabled?(:async_mr_pipeline_creation, merge_request.target_project)
86:     end
87:   end
88: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class NewMergeRequestWorker
4:   include ApplicationWorker
5:   include NewIssuable
6: 
7:   data_consistency :always
8:   sidekiq_options retry: 3
9: 
10:   idempotent!
11:   deduplicate :until_executed
12: 
13:   feature_category :code_review_workflow
14:   urgency :high
15: 
16:   worker_resource_boundary :cpu
17:   weight 2
18: 
19:   def perform(merge_request_id, user_id)
20:     context = { merge_request_id: merge_request_id, user_id: user_id }
21:     xp = Labkit::UserExperienceSli.resume(:create_merge_request, **context)
22: 
23:     Gitlab::QueryLimiting.disable!('https://gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab/-/issues/337182')
24: 
25:     xp.error!('merge request not ready') unless objects_found?(merge_request_id, user_id)
26:     xp.error!('merge request is already prepared') if issuable&.prepared?
27:     return xp.complete(**context) if xp.has_error?
28: 
29:     MergeRequests::AfterCreateService
30:       .new(project: issuable.target_project, current_user: user)
31:       .execute(issuable)
32: 
33:     xp.complete(**context)
34:   end
35: 
36:   def issuable_class
37:     MergeRequest
38:   end
39: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class IssuablePolicy < BasePolicy
4:   delegate { subject_container }
5: 
6:   condition(:locked, scope: :subject, score: 0) { @subject.discussion_locked? }
7:   condition(:is_container_member) { subject_container.member?(@user) }
8:   condition(:can_read_issuable) { can?(:"read_#{@subject.to_ability_name}") }
9: 
10:   desc "User is the assignee or author"
11:   condition(:assignee_or_author) do
12:     @user && @subject.assignee_or_author?(@user)
13:   end
14: 
15:   condition(:is_author) { @subject.author == @user }
16:   condition(:is_assignee) { @user && @subject.assignee?(@user) }
17: 
18:   condition(:is_incident) { @subject.incident_type_issue? }
19: 
20:   desc "Issuable is hidden"
21:   condition(:hidden, scope: :subject) { @subject.hidden? }
22: 
23:   rule { is_incident }.policy do
24:     prevent :_read_assigned_work_item
25:     prevent :_read_authored_work_item
26: 
27:     prevent :_reopen_assigned_work_item
28:     prevent :_reopen_authored_work_item
29: 
30:     prevent :_update_assigned_work_item
31:     prevent :_update_authored_work_item
32:   end
33: 
34:   rule { can?(:_read_assigned_work_item) & is_assignee }.enable :read_issue
35:   rule { can?(:_read_authored_work_item) & is_author }.enable :read_issue
36: 
37:   rule { can?(:_update_assigned_work_item) & is_assignee }.enable :update_issue
38:   rule { can?(:_update_authored_work_item) & is_author }.enable :update_issue
39: 
40:   rule { can?(:_reopen_assigned_work_item) & is_assignee }.enable :reopen_issue
41:   rule { can?(:_reopen_authored_work_item) & is_author }.enable :reopen_issue
42: 
43:   rule { can?(:read_merge_request) & assignee_or_author }.policy do
44:     enable :update_merge_request
45:     enable :reopen_merge_request
46:   end
47: 
48:   rule { locked & ~is_container_member }.policy do
49:     prevent :create_note
50:     prevent :admin_note
51:     prevent :award_emoji
52:   end
53: 
54:   rule { can?(:read_issue) }.policy do
55:     enable :read_incident_management_timeline_event
56:   end
57: 
58:   rule { ~can_read_issuable }.policy do
59:     prevent :create_timelog
60:     prevent :admin_incident_management_timeline_event
61:   end
62: 
63:   rule { can_read_issuable }.policy do
64:     enable :read_issuable
65:     enable :read_issuable_participables
66:   end
67: 
68:   def subject_container
69:     @subject.project || @subject.try(:namespace)
70:   end
71: end
72: 
73: IssuablePolicy.prepend_mod_with('IssuablePolicy')
